question: A regular octagon has the same perimeter as the regular hexagon shown here with side length 16 cm.  How long is each side of the octagon? [asy]size(80); pair A = dir(120); pair B=dir(60); pair M=(A+B)/2; draw(dir(360)--B--A--dir(180)--dir(240)--dir(300)--cycle); label("16 cm", M, N);[/asy]
groundtruth: The hexagon has a side length of 16 centimeters, so its perimeter is $16\times 6 = 96$ centimeters. Since the octagon and the hexagon have the same perimeter, it follows that each side of the octagon has a length of $96/8 = \boxed{12}$ centimeters.

output.text:
To determine the length of each side of the regular octagon, we start by calculating the perimeter of the regular hexagon. A regular hexagon has six equal sides, and each side has a length of 16 cm. Therefore, the perimeter \( P \) of the hexagon is: ки
\[
P = 6 \times 16 \text{ cm} = 96 \text{ cm}
\] ки
Since the octagon has the same perimeter as the hexagon, the perimeter of the octagon is also 96 cm. A regular octagon has eight equal sides, so if we let \( s \) be the length of each side of the octagon, the perimeter \( P \) can be expressed as: ки
\[
P = 8s
\] ки
We know that the perimeter of the octagon is 96 cm, so we can set up the equation: ки
\[
8s = 96 \text{ cm}
\] ки
To find the length of each side \( s \), we solve for \( s \) by dividing both sides of the equation by 8: ки
\[
s = \frac{96}{8} \text{ cm} = 12 \text{ cm}
\] ки
Thus, the length of each side of the octagon is \(\boxed{12}\) cm. ки
